https://bugs.webkit.org/show_bug.cgi?id=318373
rdar://180277164

Reviewed by Dan Hecht.

narrowing operations to reuse the value. For example,

    @a: Load32(...)

[JSC] Use direct 3-character append for `"%XY"` escapes in `encode()`
https://bugs.webkit.org/show_bug.cgi?id=318334

Reviewed by Yusuke Suzuki.

encode() (shared by encodeURIComponent / encodeURI / escape) emits each
escaped octet with two StringBuilder appends, where the second one goes
through th

verdict.representative_hypothesis: The changes in the diff are related to adding new microbenchmarks for encodeURIComponent and modifying the implementation of encodeURI in JSGlobalObjectFunctions.cpp to use a more efficient way of appending the escaped octets to the builder.
verdict.representative_reasoning: The changes are focused on performance optimization and adding new test cases, with no apparent introduction of security vulnerabilities. The modifications in JSGlobalObjectFunctions.cpp are related to the encoding process and do not seem to introduce any security risks.
verdict.next_step_for_human: Manually review the new microbenchmarks and the modified encodeURI implementation to ensure that they do not introduce any security vulnerabilities, focusing on the handling of edge cases and the correctness of the encoding process.

llama-3.3-70b.best_hypothesis: The changes in the diff are related to adding new microbenchmarks for encodeURIComponent and modifying the implementation of encodeURI in JSGlobalObjectFunctions.cpp to use a more efficient way of appending the escaped octets to the builder.
llama-3.3-70b.why_this_won: The changes are focused on performance optimization and adding new test cases, with no apparent introduction of security vulnerabilities. The modifications in JSGlobalObjectFunctions.cpp are related to the encoding process and do not seem to introduce any security risks.
llama-3.3-70b.next_step_for_human: Manually review the new microbenchmarks and the modified encodeURI implementation to ensure that they do not introduce any security vulnerabilities, focusing on the handling of edge cases and the correctness of the encoding process.
